name: Live Channels
overview: Build looping virtual TV channels that appear natively in Jellyfin's Live TV.
description: Live Channels lets administrators build looping virtual TV channels from genres and official ratings or from a hand-picked list of items. Each channel, its guide, and its stream are served in-process through Jellyfin's own Live TV, so the channels appear automatically with no tuner to add, no URLs to paste, and no exposed endpoints.

versions.changelog:
## Developer
- Serve opened streams through Jellyfin's internal live stream endpoint as a direct stream provider
- The opened stream is probed again on every server version, so playback direct streams instead of re-encoding as interlaced

## Developer
- Apply the VPP brightness gain before the tone map instead of after, matching Jellyfin's order
- Fixes washed out HDR playback when a VPP tone mapping brightness gain is set

## Developer
- Add a watchdog for tracking if there is an open channel but no sessions then closing that channel
- Prevent hanging channels and kill their ffmpeg if unused.

## Developer
- Obtain HWA device based on settings instead of falling back to 128

## Developer
- Fix a live channel dropping for the remaining viewers when one of several viewers stops watching
- Update the README for the current features

## Developer
- Add rating blocks to the Popular channel
- Tag kids per rating block
- Add News and Sports channel categories with an automatic movie tag
- Replace Extra content with Content Types toggles

## Developer
- Add time of day rating blocks with a transition window
- Add shuffle, alphabetical, and chronological loop order
- Use collections as channel sources
- Organize channel and popular settings into sub sections
- Split ChannelService into focused files

## Developer
- Bump Base to 2026.7.12
- Use ActivityLogger from Base 2026.7.12
- Standardize activity log messages
- Move Stress test and Reset into a Troubleshooting section

## Developer
- Item boundaries are now timestamp-continuous - each item's timeline chains from where the previous item actually ended instead of the scheduled boundary, so seams carry no PTS jump for a client's video decoder to stall on (seen as frozen video with continuing audio on VLCKit-based players like Swiftfin)

## Developer
- Dolby Vision Profile 5 items are now excluded from schedules with a clear log line - Profile 5 has no HDR10-compatible base layer, so it renders green/purple through any tone mapper; Profile 8 and HDR10 versions play correctly and stay included
- Schedules now heal metadata runtime drift - when a full item plays through, its real length is recorded and used from the next guide refresh, closing the timestamp gap (or overlap) at item boundaries when a file's metadata runtime is wrong

## Developer
- HDR letterbox bars are now true black on the Intel GPU pipeline - padding happens after the tone map, so the bars are painted in SDR space instead of being run through the HDR-to-SDR LUT (which rendered them green)
- HDR brightness now honours Jellyfin's VPP Tone mapping brightness/contrast gain (Dashboard > Playback > Transcoding), applied after the GPU tone map, so channels match regular playback instead of rendering dark
- Internal cleanup - removed the unused Windows QSV legacy paths (vendor-matched HDR chain, interlaced/10-bit decode workarounds), refreshed comments to describe current behaviour, and rewrote the README to be concise and current

## Developer
- First tune-in no longer stalls on large channels - freshly built schedules stay in memory after each guide refresh instead of being reloaded (or silently rebuilt) on the tune-in critical path, and any slow or failed schedule load is now visible in the log
- Subtitle burn-in now stays on the GPU for Intel hardware - the subtitle renders on the CPU and is composited in VRAM (overlay_vaapi), so Forced-only channels playing foreign-language 4K/HDR keep full GPU speed instead of dropping to the software chain
- Compatible with Jellyfin 10.11.10 - the opened-source probe (the interlace workaround) is now version-gated, fixing total playback failure on 10.11.10 servers
- Sessions tab - selecting a session opens its ffmpeg log in a modal with Refresh, Copy, and Kill; logs are cached per session and removed when the session closes; the speed column now shows the ratio next to the status dot

## Developer
- The Sessions tab shows when a viewerless stream is winding down (with its countdown), and its Kill button now stops the encoder immediately instead of waiting out the wind-down
- New encoder stress test on the Settings tab - pick a demanding library item and the server measures how many concurrent streams it can sustain with the real channel pipeline, recommending the Maximum concurrent streams value
- Fully GPU-resident Intel pipeline (Linux QSV/VAAPI) - decode, deinterlace, scale, tone map, and encode all stay in VRAM on Jellyfin's configured render node. Benchmarked on an N100 iGPU with 4K HDR10 HEVC, 2.6x faster than the old download-to-CPU chain; the Intel 10-bit and interlaced software-decode workarounds are gone, and HDR tone maps scale-first (1.9x faster, same picture)

## Developer
- Channels now play with a lossless remux instead of a full re-encode, fixing the false Interlaced video is not supported transcode Jellyfin forces on every live-TV stream (the opened stream is probed so the real, progressive format wins)
- Much faster channel start-up, with the client buffer filling at full speed instead of realtime
- Streams no longer fall behind over long watches - every encoder start bursts exactly the wall-clock time the session has lost, so item transitions self-heal
- Fixed a playlist write race that could crash Jellyfin's stream reader (ffmpeg exit 139) and kill playback mid-session
- Channels degrade gracefully - standby appears within seconds when a library is unreachable, and a repeatedly failing encoder backs off instead of relaunching forever
- Removed the pre-render/warm-splice pipeline and the playback buffer, pre-render, stream window, and producer rate settings - pacing, buffering, and the disk window are now automatic and self-tuning
- Item transitions cut segments exactly on the 4-second boundary (2-second GOP alignment)
- Re-tuning a channel within 30 seconds is now instant - the still-warm encoder is adopted by the new stream instead of cold-starting a fresh one
- Faster tune-in - the stream probe analysis window was halved, and the channel logo no longer resolves before the encoder starts

## Developer
- Cleanup & 8.2 preparation

## Features
- Give every channel real variety, each series plays one block then steps aside until the others have aired, cycling through the series over time so no show dominates
- Make the Popular channel show 10 distinct series and 25 distinct movies instead of one big show filling it
- Build Popular from what the server has watched most recently, so the channel follows your latest viewing
- Keep only genuine two-part episodes together, extending a block by at most one episode

## Features
- Export and import your channels from the Channels tab, so you can copy a setup between servers
- Keep channels varied by never repeating a series until every other series has aired, so one big show no longer dominates the loop
- Make the Popular channel more diverse by choosing shows from your most-watched episodes and rotating a larger pool of movies and shows

## Features
- Find studios, people, and the audio or subtitle language by searching instead of scrolling a long list, so any studio such as HBO is reachable
- Group the channel content filters into their own Filters section for a tidier editor

## Features
- Filter a channel by production year or decade, so you can build a 90s channel
- Filter a channel by studio or network, like an HBO channel, matching shows by their series too
- Filter a channel by people, so you can build a favourite actor or director channel
- Filter a channel by a minimum community or critic rating, for a best-of channel

## Features
- Start channels quickly even right after a restart by saving each item's video details with the schedule, so tune-in no longer re-scans the library
- Keep each channel's schedule in its own file and release it from memory once the channel stops, so large setups use less memory
- Add a pre-render setting to control how early the next item starts encoding, which can be lowered or turned off to save CPU on low-power hardware

## Features
- Build each channel schedule once and reuse it so large channels start quickly instead of failing to load

## Features
- Play 10 bit content on Intel by decoding it in software so it no longer fails to start
- Restore hardware accelerated playback on Intel after the previous build left it unable to start

## Features
- Run the whole Intel QSV and VAAPI pipeline on the GPU the way Jellyfin does, so interlaced and 10-bit content plays instead of failing to start
- Add a producer rate setting that feeds the player at realtime by default and can be raised to keep the server from falling behind
- Limit the plugin API to administrators so only the dashboard can list or close live sessions
- Close the encoder when a tune in is cancelled so an abandoned stream stops leaking

## Features
- Play interlaced content on Intel QSV and VAAPI by decoding it in software for the whole file, so it no longer fails to start
- Run the producer a little above realtime so it can recover a boundary gap and never become the bottleneck

## Features
- Add a per channel option to include home videos and other loose video files alongside movies, episodes, and music videos
- Start large channels far faster by skipping a full per item HDR scan when it cannot change how the channel plays

## Features
- Add a Sessions tab that lists every active stream with its channel, start time, run time, and live encode speed, and a button to stop it
- Warm up the next item while the current one is still playing so per-item channels switch files without a gap
- Cap how many channels stream at once so a client that never disconnects cannot pile up encoders and overload the server
- Add a stream time limit that closes any channel left running too long as a backstop for clients that never send a stop
- Organise the Settings tab into collapsible sections

## Features
- Give the Popular channel its own tab to set its name, icon, rating limit, subtitle rule, and loop behaviour
- Build the Popular channel from your recent, top rated, and most watched movies and shows
- Measure most watched across the whole server by combining every user's play counts
- Choose a default language in Settings that decides when Forced subtitles burn in

## Features
- Add a built in Popular channel that loops your top movies and shows blended with recent additions
- Fill the guide with episode titles, ratings, original air dates, and an HD marker
- Show landscape artwork in the guide, backdrops for movies and thumbnails for episodes and music videos

## Features
- Exclude genres from a channel, with series level genres applied across their episodes
- Limit a channel to a single audio language by its default audio track
- Set the playback buffer length and the stream file location in Settings
- Clean up leftover stream files with a scheduled task in Live TV

## Fixes
- Stream channels as a rolling playlist of small segments so disk use stays bounded no matter how long they run
- Pre-roll a few seconds on tune-in so playback starts on a full buffer
- Tidy the channel editor genre dropdown and favor layout

## Fixes
- Remove the trim and zero disk settings that could cause stutter

## Fixes
- Decode 10-bit HEVC and 4K sources on the GPU instead of falling back to software

## Fixes
- Build channels internally through Jellyfin Live TV again so playback works
- Keep the stream building ahead of playback to reduce stutter

## Fixes
- Run the Intel pipeline the same way Jellyfin does, decoding on VAAPI and encoding on QSV, so 10-bit and HDR channels play
- Read the GPU render device from the Jellyfin configuration and log the producer command

## Fixes
- Pin the Intel iHD driver on the VAAPI device so the GPU pipeline initialises on multi-GPU systems
- Log the producer command so playback failures are visible

## Fixes
- Rebuild the Intel decode pipeline to run entirely on the GPU through VAAPI, fixing 10-bit and HDR playback
- Channels start fast again instead of probing every item on tune-in

## Fixes
- Decode 10-bit sources in software so they no longer fail on QSV

## Features
- Serve channels through a self configuring M3U tuner with an XMLTV guide
- Channel logos and program artwork in the guide

## Fixes
- Remove the old native channels

## Fixes
- Serve channels as an M3U tuner on a private loopback port

## Fixes
- Stop abandoned channel sessions from piling up

## Fixes
- Forced subtitles now show for non-English audio

## Fixes
- Activity cleanup
- Better subtitle defaults

## Fixes
- Use Jellyfin's default audio track instead of assuming

## Features
- File trimming
- Prioritize file types

## Fixes
- Codec fixes

## Fixes
- Codec fixes

## Fixes
- Smaller file work to make music videos actually work
- HDR nonsense

## Fixes
- Even more QSV nonsense...

## Fixes
- Even more QSV nonsense

## Fixes
- Even more QSV nonsense

## Fixes
- More QSV nonsense

## Fixes
- QSV nonsense

## Fixes
- HWA decode fixes for non-MacOS devices
- Icon fixes

## Initial Release!

This plugin exists because I need to test Live TV for Swiftfin and I wanted a simple(r) tuner configuration as I found a lot of the alternatives to be a little too involved for what I wanted.
